title: cyanus nissanus
side_effects: Cyanus nissanus can have side effects if used improperly, such as rapid heartbeat, dizziness, and nausea due to its potent alkaloid compounds. Ingestion of large quantities can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, causing dehydration and electrolyte imbalance. Prolonged exposure to its toxic properties can also result in liver and kidney damage, potentially causing long-term health complications. If not handled correctly, Cyanus nissanus can cause severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, in individuals with sensitive immune systems.
